title: Quiz: Quantum Speedup Analysis
description: Test your understanding of quantum speedup conditions, resource requirements, and practical feasibility

questions.question: What is the theoretical speedup of quantum Monte Carlo over classical Monte Carlo?
questions.explanation: Quantum Monte Carlo provides quadratic speedup via amplitude estimation. Classical MC needs O(1/ε²) samples for accuracy ε, while quantum needs O(1/ε) queries - the speedup factor is O(1/ε) itself.

questions.question: What can potentially negate the quantum speedup in Monte Carlo applications?
questions.explanation: If state preparation requires O(2^n) gates to load the probability distribution, this exponential cost dominates and can completely negate the quadratic speedup from amplitude estimation. Efficient (polynomial) state preparation is essential.

questions.question: What is the typical overhead factor from quantum error correction?
questions.explanation: Current estimates for fault-tolerant quantum computing with surface codes suggest 100-1000x overhead in physical qubits and gate operations compared to ideal logical operations. This significantly impacts the practical speedup.

questions.question: For which type of probability distribution can quantum state preparation be done efficiently?

options.text: No distributions can be loaded efficiently

questions.explanation: Log-concave distributions, which include normal and lognormal distributions commonly used in finance, can be loaded efficiently using algorithms like Grover-Rudolph with polynomial gate complexity O(n²).

questions.question: With current quantum hardware (2024), quantum Monte Carlo can already outperform classical computers for option pricing.
questions.explanation: False. Current NISQ devices lack the qubit count, gate fidelity, and error correction needed for practical advantage. The overhead from errors and limited coherence time means classical computers still outperform quantum for practical Monte Carlo problems.

questions.question: What accuracy level is typically needed for quantum Monte Carlo to break even with classical?

options.text: Quantum is always faster regardless of accuracy

questions.explanation: Due to the significant overhead from error correction and state preparation, quantum advantage only manifests at very high accuracy requirements (ε ~ 10^-4 to 10^-6). At lower accuracy, the overhead dominates and classical is faster.

questions.question: What is the estimated timeline for practical quantum advantage in Monte Carlo for finance?
questions.explanation: Most estimates suggest 2030-2040 for practical quantum advantage in financial Monte Carlo. This requires millions of physical qubits, low error rates (~10^-6), and mature error correction - expected to take 5-15 years from current technology.

questions.question: Which factors contribute to the total cost of quantum Monte Carlo? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Quantum MC costs include: (a) state preparation, (b) payoff oracle, and (c) Grover iterations. Classical random number generation (d) is not part of quantum MC - randomness comes from quantum mechanics itself.
